destination.vibe: A 62,000-person mountain town where Chinese Nationalist soldiers stranded after Mao's 1949 victory planted oolong tea on 1,200-meter ridges and built a Yunnan village that has nothing to do with Thailand — and a 108km road loop climbing 1,700 meters through it waits 40km from your guesthouse, morning clear before the rains arrive.

flights.outbound_dates: July–August flexible (Tue/Wed departures cheapest; book 6–8 weeks out)
flights.notes: DTW → ICN (Seoul Incheon) → BKK (Bangkok Suvarnabhumi). Then add domestic: Thai AirAsia or Nok Air BKK (Don Mueang DMK) → CEI, ~$30–45 each way. Korean Air accepts bikes as checked baggage ($200 each way).

flights.outbound_dates: July–August flexible (midweek Tue/Wed best; book 6–8 weeks out)
flights.notes: DTW → TPE (Taipei Taoyuan) → BKK. Add domestic BKK → CEI separately (~$30–45). EVA bike policy: confirm at evaair.com during booking.

flights.outbound_dates: August (slightly cheaper than July; book 6–8 weeks out)
flights.notes: DTW → HKG (Hong Kong) → BKK. Add domestic BKK → CEI separately. Bike policy: accepted as sports equipment, fees apply.

lodging.name: Bed & Bike Hostel (Poshtel)
lodging.neighborhood: Chiang Rai city center — near Clock Tower
lodging.highlights: The only explicitly cycling-dedicated accommodation in Chiang Rai — bike hire, secure bike storage, coffee shop on-site. Private rooms from ~$25–30/night. Walking distance to the night market.

lodging.name: Wiang Inn Hotel
lodging.neighborhood: Chiang Rai city center — 5 min walk to night market
lodging.highlights: Classic mid-range choice — well-maintained rooms, outdoor pool, free breakfast, free parking. On Phahonyothin Road, the main artery heading north toward the Mae Salong climb.

lodging.name: The Legend Chiang Rai Boutique River Resort & Spa
lodging.neighborhood: Kok River bank — 10 min from city center
lodging.highlights: Boutique bungalow resort on the Kok River — 78 Northern Thai-style rooms through gardens, spa, pool, riverside dining. Bike rentals on-site.

itinerary.overview: Chiang Rai sits at 580 meters in Thailand's far north, surrounded by mountain ranges pushing to 1,600 meters along the borders with Myanmar and Laos. A working provincial town of 62,000, not a resort city — the vibe is closer to a Thai county seat than a tourist destination. The cycling culture is real and the road infrastructure heading north is excellent. July is rainy season: reliable morning windows (7am–noon, often clear) followed by afternoon thunderstorms. Start early, climb before the clouds build, be descending by noon.

days.title: Doi Mae Salong Loop — 108km / 67mi, 1,729m climbing
days.morning: Wheels by 7am north on Route 1 toward Mae Chan (34km, flat warmup). Turn west onto Route 1130 and climb 25km through rubber plantations and tea gardens to Santikhiri (Mae Salong) at 1,200m — a surreal Yunnan Chinese enclave founded by KMT 93rd Division soldiers who fled Mao's China in 1949. Streets in Chinese characters, Yunnan Mandarin spoken, oolong tea on terraced hillsides. Stop for tea and khao soi before the descent.
days.afternoon: Descend the south side via Route 1234 toward Ban Hin Taek, then east through Wiang Pa Pao and south to close the loop back to Chiang Rai. Be back in town by 1pm. Afternoon: Wat Rong Khun (the White Temple) 13km south — an entirely white glass-mosaic Buddhist temple with a causeway over a sea of sculpted hands.
days.evening: Night Bazaar in the city center (6–11pm). Smaller and more local than Chiang Mai: khao soi, larb moo, sai oua sausage, sticky rice. Dinner ~$5–7.

days.title: Doi Tung Climb + Golden Triangle — 95km / 59mi, 1,300m climbing
days.morning: Depart 6:30am north on Route 110 toward Mae Sai (65km). At the Doi Tung junction (35km north), turn west onto the mountain road. Climb 18km to 1,389m through the Mae Fah Luang Royal Project — former opium-production zone transformed by the late Princess Mother into macadamia and tea farms. The Mae Fah Luang Garden at the summit has 100+ acres of ornamental planting, teak Royal Villa, views into Myanmar. Allow 45 minutes.
days.afternoon: Descend north through Mae Sai border town. Continue east along Route 1016 — the Mekong River Road — to Chiang Saen and the Golden Triangle. The Hall of Opium museum (800 THB, 2 hours) at Sop Ruak is one of Southeast Asia's best: a serious institutional history of the opium trade, built by the Royal Project that eradicated opium in these hills. The context of looking out at the actual Golden Triangle while reading this history is surreal.
days.evening: Riverside dinner in Sop Ruak watching longtail boats on the Mekong between three countries. Return to Chiang Rai by songthaew or van (~60km). Dinner: Phu Lae restaurant on Trairat Road for northern Thai naem prik noom and larb neua.

days.title: Chiang Saen River Loop via Mekong Road — 80km / 50mi, 400m climbing
days.morning: Recovery/exploration day. North on Route 1016 through rice paddies and market villages, Mekong on the left. Chiang Saen (55km): the Lanna Kingdom's former capital, now a quiet town of 5,000 with intact walls and moat, Wat Chedi Luang ruins (1328, 58m chedi), small National Museum. Lock the bike and walk the ruins 30 minutes.
days.afternoon: Continue 11km to Sop Ruak for the Golden Triangle viewpoint in morning light, then south back toward Chiang Rai. Optional extension to Chiang Khong (40km further north, most isolated Mekong stretch) if legs are there — then van back. Otherwise be home by noon.
days.evening: Final night: Saturday Night Walking Street on Thanalai Road if timing works (6–11pm, local food and hill-tribe vendors). Otherwise return to the best stall you missed on Day 1.

tips: July rain timing: mornings clear until about 11am–noon, afternoon thunderstorms most days 2–6pm. Start all rides by 7am and plan your descent or return by 11:30am.

Mae Salong grades: up to 22–24% on the steepest ramps, short and punchy. Route 1130 surface is well-maintained. Clockwise (north side up, south side down via Ban Hin Taek) offers longer but more consistently graded climbing.

Bike logistics on the domestic leg: Thai AirAsia and Nok Air accept bikes as checked baggage on BKK→CEI. Book the sports baggage allowance when purchasing your ticket online — adding it at the airport costs more.

KMT history of Mae Salong: the 93rd Division crossed into Burma after the 1949 Communist victory and spent years fighting through the Golden Triangle borderlands before Thailand offered them land in exchange for fighting Communist insurgents. The old men who speak Yunnan Mandarin and play mah-jong have a story not written in any guidebook.
Hall of Opium (Sop Ruak): funded by the Thai royal family, staffed by serious historians. Allow 2 full hours minimum. ~800 THB (~$22).
Hydration: July mountain humidity means you'll sweat more than expected. Carry electrolyte tabs — Thai 7-Eleven (everywhere, including mountain villages) stocks Pocari Sweat and coconut water.

sources.title: Doi Mae Salong Chiang Rai road ride — Bicycle Thailand
Cycling Thailand: 3 beautiful days around Chiang Rai — Stefan in Thailand
The Golden Frontier Chiang Rai Tour — Cycling Chiang Mai
Best road biking trails in Chiang Rai — AllTrails

destination.vibe: Home turf of Tadej Pogačar — where 50 cobbled hairpins climb to 1,611 meters on a road built by Russian WWI prisoners, the Soča runs glacier-turquoise through raw limestone gorges, and summer temperatures in the low 20s mean you can push 80-mile days in the Julian Alps without a 5am alarm.

flights.outbound_dates: July–August flexible (Tue/Wed departures cheapest; book 4–6 weeks out)
flights.notes: Connects through Frankfurt (FRA→LJU). Bike fee €100 each way (Economy). Deflate tires, remove pedals, lower saddle.
Connects through Vienna (VIE→LJU, ~1h — shortest final leg of any routing). Bike fee €75–100 each way.
Connects through Zurich (ZRH→LJU, ~1h30m). Bike fee €80–100 each way.

lodging.name: Reset Apartments — Bike Resort Kranjska Gora
lodging.highlights: Officially designated 'Bike Resort' — secure indoor bike storage, wash station, drying room. Walking distance to the Vršič Pass road start.

lodging.name: Hotel Kompas Kranjska Gora
lodging.highlights: Slovenia's premier cyclist-specialist hotel — certified cycling accommodation with secure storage, assembly area, pool and wellness. Walking distance to Vršič climb start.

lodging.name: Hotel Cubo Ljubljana
lodging.neighborhood: Ljubljana Old Town — 400m from Triple Bridge
lodging.highlights: Cycle-friendly certified, confirmed secure bike storage. Perfect first/last night in the city before heading to the Julian Alps.

itinerary.overview: Ljubljana sits at the foot of the Julian Alps, 50km from roads that shaped Tadej Pogačar. Within a day's ride you're at 1,611 meters on the Vršič Pass — 50 hairpin turns, cobbled bends 5–9, and the Soča Valley glacier-turquoise below. July temperatures average 20–25°C at valley level. No 5am alarms required.

days.title: Ljubljana → Bled via Sava Valley — 75 miles / 120km, 1,200m climbing
days.morning: Roll from the Dragon Bridge northwest through Šiška district along the Sava River. Stop in Kranj (32km) — Slovenia's 4th city on a limestone peninsula above the gorge. Continue through Lesce to the Radovna Valley approach to Bled: almost no traffic, beech forest, first views of Triglav National Park. Ljubljana to Bled: ~60km.
days.afternoon: Lake Bled circuit (6km, flat). Climb the short access road to Ojstrica viewpoint (20% pitch, unpaved last 100m, best overhead Bled view). Optional Pokljuka Plateau extension: 18km from Bled, 800m gain, 1,330m summit, biathlon venue.
days.evening: Dinner at Gostilna Pri Planincu (Grajska cesta 8) — local prices, good trout and struklji. Then up to Bled Castle (130m cliff, lit at night) for the view of the Alps you'll spend the next two days riding through.

days.title: Vršič Pass Queen Stage — 88km / 55 miles, 1,900m climbing
days.morning: Start Kranjska Gora 7am. The Vršič climb (12km to 1,611m): bends 5–9 are the famous cobbled section at 15–17% on original 1916 WWI prisoner-built road surface. Pause at bend #8 for the Russian Orthodox Chapel — 400 soldiers killed by avalanche here during construction. Summit: 50 hairpins, the most of any pass in Europe, Triglav's north face visible.
days.afternoon: Descend 26 hairpins into Trenta Valley (Soča side, steeper). Detour 3km to the Soča source — cold blue-green pool erupting from a limestone grotto. Follow the valley floor 26km to Bovec along the glacier-blue Soča through the Great Soča Gorge.
days.evening: Lunch in Bovec (Bovška trout, polenta). Wade into the Soča: 8–12°C river is the best ice-bath in the Alps. Dinner at Gostilna Sovdat for traditional Primorska cuisine.

days.title: Mangart Saddle — Slovenia's Highest Drivable Road — 90km / 56 miles, 2,200m climbing
days.morning: Depart Bovec 7am. Koritnica Valley junction (6km), turn east toward the Mangart toll gate (~€10 cars; cyclists waved through or nominal fee). Climb 10km to 2,072m: five dark rock tunnels requiring a front light, grades to 25% on the upper ramps, views extending into Austria and Italy from the saddle.
days.afternoon: Descend to Bovec. Continue south to Kobarid (12km) — site of Hemingway's A Farewell to Arms. The Kobarid Museum (European Museum of the Year 1993) is one of the best WWI museums in Europe. Allow 90 minutes. Lunch: Hiša Franko (Ana Roš's restaurant, 4km north) if you have a reservation.
days.evening: Return to Kranjska Gora or Ljubljana for flight connection. Kranjska Gora to LJU is 85km by road (1h15m).

tips: Vršič cobbled hairpins (bends 5–9): commit to your gear before the cobbles start — a shift at 15% on rough stone can snap a chain. Wide tires (28–32c) handle this surface more confidently.
Mangart tunnel lighting is mandatory: five dark tunnels, longest ~90 seconds at climbing pace.
Alpine afternoon weather: July thunderstorms develop fast over Triglav by 1–2pm. Start before 7:30am, be off exposed ridges by noon.
Soča River recovery: 8–12°C even in July. A 10-minute wade after Day 2 is the best ice-bath you'll find at altitude.

sources.title: Cycling Slovenia's Julian Alps: routes, GPS files, hotels + more — Epic Road Rides
Slovenia Road Bike Tour — Best of Slovenia in 8 Days — 57hours
Vršič Pass climb stats — Pjamm Cycling

destination.vibe: The flattest big city in Europe, wrapped in Moorish palaces and orange trees, with 180km of dedicated bike lanes connecting neighborhoods that haven't changed in 500 years — you just have to be on the road before the sun gets serious.

flights.notes: Connects through Istanbul (IST). Bike fees typically €50–80 each way in a hard case.
Connects through Madrid (MAD→SVQ, 1h15m shuttle). Bike fees €50–100 each way.
Connects through Amsterdam (AMS→SVQ ~3h). Bike fee €50–75 each way.

lodging.name: La Banda Rooftop Hostel
lodging.highlights: Steps from Torre del Oro and Guadalquivir riverfront. Confirm secure indoor bike storage at check-in. Rooftop terrace with Cathedral views.

lodging.name: Hotel Palacio Alcázar
lodging.highlights: 19th-century palace 200m from the Real Alcázar walls. Interior parking for bikes. 4.3/5 on Booking.

lodging.name: Hotel Corral del Rey
lodging.highlights: 17th-century merchant's townhouse, four courtyards, 13 rooms. Secure bike storage. The most atmospheric address in Seville.

itinerary.overview: Seville is one of the great surprises of European cycling — 180km of separated bike lanes through a city of Moorish palaces, orange trees, and flamenco bars. Completely flat. The catch: July temperatures hit 40–43°C by noon. All serious cycling must happen before 9am or after 6:30pm.

destination.vibe: The birthplace of democracy, tragedy, and the gyro — where a 2,500-year-old citadel glows gold at sunset over a city of five million, a souvlaki costs €4, and nothing serious happens before 9pm.

lodging.name: City Circus Athens
lodging.highlights: 9.6/10 on Hostelworld. Neoclassical building, steps from Monastiraki Metro. 2026 Hoscar Award winner.

lodging.name: Sweet Home Hotel
lodging.highlights: 9.3/10 on Booking (1,600 reviews). Elegant rooms 400m from Syntagma Square, some with Acropolis views, homemade breakfast.

lodging.name: AthensWas Design Hotel
lodging.highlights: 21 rooms on the pedestrian promenade directly below the Acropolis with unobstructed Parthenon views.

itinerary.overview: Athens is not a museum with a city around it — it's a city of five million that happens to have an intact 5th-century BC temple on its central hill.

destination.vibe: The city that taught Europe to eat — 900 years of university life, the world's longest portico, tagliatelle al ragù that has nothing to do with 'spaghetti bolognese,' and terracotta buildings so red they named a political color after them.

The city that went from narco capital to Most Innovative City on Earth in one generation — and is now doing it again with food, coffee, street art, and a metro system that's become a point of civic pride.
No nonstops, no problem — Bangkok rewards the extra hours in transit with 12 million people, temples against skyscrapers, Michelin street food for $1, and the baht weaker than it's been in years.
Twenty-two million people, the world's best street tacos, Diego Rivera murals on government buildings, Frida's blue house, Aztec ruins under a baroque cathedral, mezcal bars in converted mansions.

Seven hills, a 1755 earthquake that flattened everything and got rebuilt fast, 600 years of seafaring bravado, and the most melancholic pop music genre ever invented — all held together by custard tarts and cheap wine.
A city that does everything well and brags about none of it — temples next to bubble tea next to Michelin stalls next to the world's best convenience stores.
Ten million people, 600 years of history, and the world's most obsessive food culture — all running at 5G.
